active_description: Automate system administration workflows on macOS and Linux. Use this skill when: setting up reproducible dev environments (Brewfile), parsing CLI command output programmatically (jc + jq), monitoring remote servers via SSH with alerts, integrating API keys and secrets securely (1Password CLI), building automation workflows (Cherri Siri Shortcuts programming), or batch-managing system configurations. Covers package management (Homebrew), structured JSON parsing (jc), SSH-based remote administration, secure credential injection, cross-platform multi-step automation, and fleet monitoring.

history.description: Administer macOS and Linux systems via CLI using jc for JSON-parsed command output, Homebrew for reproducible package management, 1Password CLI for secure credential handling, and Cherri for automation workflows. Use this skill when managing local or remote infrastructure, setting up team development environments, automating cross-platform system tasks, or integrating secrets into scripts. Covers SSH-based remote administration, structured CLI output parsing with jq, secrets rotation, and multi-step automation.
weaknesses: No explicit action verbs tied to tools
Does not name Brewfile, SSH alerts, or macOS admin commands
Generic phrasing ('managing', 'automating') overlaps with many other skills

history.description: Automate system administration workflows on macOS and Linux. Use this skill when: setting up reproducible dev environments (Brewfile), parsing CLI command output programmatically (jc + jq), monitoring remote servers via SSH with alerts, integrating API keys and secrets securely (1Password CLI), building automation workflows (Cherri Siri Shortcuts programming), or batch-managing system configurations. Covers package management (Homebrew), structured JSON parsing (jc), SSH-based remote administration, secure credential injection, cross-platform multi-step automation, and fleet monitoring.
improvements: Explicit use-case list with parenthetical tool references
Named Brewfile, Cherri, 1Password CLI with action context
Fleet monitoring and batch configs added as boundary terms
Should-trigger rate expected: 90%+ on train set, 80%+ on validation
history.boundary_risk: Low — 'jc', 'Brewfile', 'Cherri', and '1Password CLI' are specific enough to avoid matching generic CLI or Docker questions

validation_set_boundary_risks.risk: SSH triggers on generic networking questions
validation_set_boundary_risks.mitigation: SSH is paired with 'remote servers + alerts' context, not general networking
1Password CLI is named specifically, not just 'secrets' or 'passwords'

next_iteration_candidates.rationale: Currently only in body text — may miss macOS sysadmin queries about system settings
eval-06 (macOS settings standardization) needs clearer trigger signal
